d660.sentence: Edinburgh , April 27, 1865 “ god moves in a mysterious way .” It is with a profoundly solemn sense of the inscrutable wonder-workings of Providence that we announce to-day the assassination of President Lincoln.
We do not and we will not discuss at present—because we have no certain information on the subject—the circumstances originating the assassination of President Lincoln.
It may have been the result of a southern conspiracy—assassination being a crime almost unknown in the north, and unfortunately too well known in the south—or it may have been the work of a madman.

d664.sentence: The assassination of President Lincoln is, without doubt, the most horrible catastrophe which has occurred within the memory of man.

The assassination of the President of the United States will, as far as we can forecast the future, be attended with no such terrible results, and the reason is easily to be discovered.
